commonProblems.h2: Nissan Silvia Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Nissan Silvia's SR20DET and CA18DET turbo engines deliver iconic performance but face well-documented failure points. NVCS rattle, timing chain stretch, and head gasket failure dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: SR20DET NVCS Rattle - Why S14/S15 Silvia Engines Make Ticking Noises
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Silvia S14 K's, S15 Spec R, S15 Spec S (1993-2002, SR20DET Blacktop)
problems.rootCause: NVCS (Nissan Variable Cam System) solenoid or sprocket wear causes oil pressure loss, creating metallic rattling from cylinder head.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty NVCS solenoid, flush oil system, reset ECU codes
repairOptions.longevity: May resolve rattle if caught early; not permanent fix if sprocket worn

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace NVCS sprocket, timing chain, tensioner and guides
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause; recommended if rattle persists after solenoid swap

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt SR20DET with upgraded NVCS components, MLS gasket, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution; includes 12-month warranty, upgraded internals for reliability

problems.recommendation: If the rattle is mild and appears only on cold start, try solenoid replacement first. Persistent rattling under load indicates sprocket wear - at that point, a rebuilt engine with upgraded NVCS components offers better long-term value for S15 Spec R owners.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt SR20DET engine prices for your Nissan Silvia

problems.h4: CA18DET Timing Chain Stretch - Why Early S13 Silvia Engines Rattle
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Silvia S13 K's, 180SX Type X (1988-1994, CA18DET)
problems.rootCause: Timing chain stretches with age, tensioner loses pressure, causing chain slap against guides and potential valve timing drift.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace hydraulic tensioner and chain guides, adjust timing
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix if chain not severely stretched; monitor closely

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides, sprockets; reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause; recommended preventative service at 80k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt CA18DET with upgraded timing components, MLS gasket
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage S13; includes warranty and reliability upgrades

problems.recommendation: For S13 owners under 80k miles, preventative timing chain service is cost-effective. Beyond 90k miles or with audible rattle, a rebuilt CA18DET often represents better value than major repair work on an ageing platform.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CA18DET Nissan Silvia engine replacement

problems.h4: SR20DET/CA18DET Head Gasket Failure - Overheating & White Smoke
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Silvia S13/S14/S15 all turbo variants (1988-2002, SR20DET/CA18DET)
problems.rootCause: Thermal stress from high boost, inadequate cooling, or ageing gasket material causes failure between cylinder and coolant passage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace gasket, resurface if needed, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Risk of recurrence if block/head warped; not recommended for high-boost use

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with MLS Gasket
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with multi-layer steel gasket, upgraded studs, pressure tested
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution; MLS gasket handles boost stress; includes 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: Head gasket failure on turbo Silvia engines rarely has a reliable minor fix. Given the labour cost of head removal and risk of warped components, a rebuilt engine with MLS gasket and upgraded hardware typically offers better long-term value and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare Silvia engine replacement prices with MLS gasket upgrade

problems.h4: SR20DET Turbocharger Failure - Loss of Boost & Oil Smoke
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Silvia S13/S14/S15 turbo variants (1988-2002, SR20DET)
problems.rootCause: Turbo bearing wear from oil starvation, heat soak, or age causes shaft play, seal failure, and loss of boost pressure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, replace bearings/seals, balance, reinstall
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if housing undamaged; requires specialist turbo rebuilder

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt SR20DET with refurbished/upgraded turbo, new oil lines
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage or modified cars; includes warranty and reliability upgrades

problems.recommendation: If turbo failure occurs on a high-mileage SR20DET, replacing the entire engine with a rebuilt unit including a refurbished turbo often costs less than standalone turbo replacement plus associated labour. For modified cars, consider a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo specification.
problems.cta: Get quotes for SR20DET engine with upgraded turbo

problems.h4: SR20DET CAS Failure - Cranks But Won't Start
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Silvia S13/S14/S15 all SR20DET variants (1991-2002)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 80,000-120,000 miles (can fail earlier with heat exposure)
problems.rootCause: Crank Angle Sensor internal circuit failure or connector corrosion prevents ECU from detecting crank position, causing no-spark/no-fuel condition.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace CAS unit, verify 180-degree alignment, clear ECU codes
repairOptions.longevity: Effective fix if sensor is sole issue; use OEM or high-quality aftermarket

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Test CAS, wiring harness, ECU inputs; replace faulty components
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if CAS replacement doesn't resolve no-start; rules out wiring issues

problems.recommendation: CAS failure is a common no-start cause on SR20DET engines. Start with sensor replacement and alignment check. If problem persists, pursue full electrical diagnostics before considering engine replacement - this is typically a repairable fault.
problems.cta: Find Nissan Silvia CAS replacement specialists UK

problems.h4: SR20DET Coil Pack Failure - Misfire Under Boost
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Silvia S13/S14/S15 all SR20DET variants (1991-2002)
problems.rootCause: Individual ignition coil packs degrade with heat cycling, causing weak spark under boost pressure and cylinder misfire.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Identify faulty coil via diagnostic scan, replace unit, clear codes
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective for isolated failure; use OEM or Splitfire upgrade

repairOptions.tier: Preventative - Full Coil Pack Set Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four coil packs and spark plugs as preventative maintenance
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage or track-use cars; reduces future misfire risk

problems.recommendation: Coil failure is the most common cause of misfire under boost on SR20DET engines. Start with diagnostic scan to identify faulty cylinder. For cars over 80k miles or used for track/drift, replacing all coils preventatively offers better reliability than reactive single-coil swaps.
problems.cta: Compare SR20DET ignition coil replacement prices UK

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Nissan Silvia
finalCta.paragraph: From NVCS rattle to head gasket failure, Silvia engine problems are well-documented but manageable with the right approach. EngineMarket connects you with trusted UK specialists who understand SR20DET and CA18DET platforms. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing known failure points, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices from multiple suppliers and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Nissan Silvia Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Nissan Silvia and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Nissan Silvia Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Nissan Silvia is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Nissan Silvia 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8L CA18DET turbo powered early S13 Silvia models (1988-1991), offering lighter weight than the later SR20 but with well-documented timing chain and head gasket vulnerabilities. Popular for budget drift builds and S13 restorations.
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Silvia 1.8L petrol engines

items.title: Nissan Silvia 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L SR20DET turbo is the definitive Silvia engine, fitted across late S13, all S14 and S15 generations. Redtop (no VVT) and Blacktop (NVCS VVT) variants offer 205-250bhp with strong aftermarket support for performance builds.
items.cta: Compare Nissan Silvia 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Nissan Silvia 2.0L Petrol Engines (Naturally Aspirated)
items.description: The non-turbo SR20DE powered Q's trim Silvia models across all generations. Lower output but simpler, more reliable operation makes it popular for budget replacements or NA-focused builds.
commonFailurePoints: less complex failure profile than turbo variants
items.cta: Compare Nissan Silvia 2.0L naturally aspirated engine prices

items.title: Nissan Silvia 2.5L Petrol Engines (Swap Applications)
items.description: The RB25DET 2.5L straight-six turbo from Skyline is a popular swap into S13/S14/S15 chassis for high-power builds. Requires custom mounts, wiring and ECU work but delivers smooth 250bhp+ potential.
commonFailurePoints: Oil pump drive failure on high RPM use
custom install complexity if not professionally fitted
items.cta: Enquire about Nissan Silvia 2.5L swap engine availability

engineYears.h2: Nissan Silvia Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Nissan Silvia's UK production run, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: S13 Early Generation: CA18DET Turbo Launch
years.description: The Silvia S13 launched with the lightweight CA18DET turbo, establishing the platform's drift credentials but introducing timing chain vulnerabilities.
keyChanges: S13 chassis introduced with CA18DET 1.8L turbo (169bhp)
Q's naturally aspirated trim launched with SR20DE
mainEngines: CA18DET - 1.8L petrol turbo (S13 K's early, 180SX early)
knownFor: Lightweight CA18DET ideal for drift builds but timing chain prone to stretch
Head gasket failure common under boost or poor cooling
Strong aftermarket parts availability for S13 chassis
years.cta: Get quotes for 1988-1991 Nissan Silvia replacement engines
years.ticker: S13 CA18DET timing chain rattle • S13 head gasket replacement cost • 180SX engine swap options

years.preview: S13 Late Generation: SR20DET Redtop Arrives
years.description: Late S13 models received the legendary SR20DET Redtop, establishing the 2.0L turbo as the definitive Silvia engine with stronger bottom end and aftermarket potential.
keyChanges: SR20DET Redtop introduced (no VVT, T25 turbo, 205bhp)
CA18DET continued in early S13/180SX models
mainEngines: SR20DET Redtop - 2.0L petrol turbo (S13 K's late, 180SX late)
CA18DET - 1.8L petrol turbo (S13 K's early carryover)

faq.h2: Nissan Silvia Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Nissan Silvia engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Nissan Silvia engines typically cost £1,500-£4,500 depending on engine code and variant. CA18DET units start around £1,500, while S15 Spec R SR20DET rebuilt engines reach £4,500.
keyPoints: CA18DET rebuilt: £1,500-£2,200 supply only
SR20DET rebuilt: £2,200-£4,500 depending on generation
Used engines available from £800 but verify compression first
Supply & fit labour adds £800-£2,500 depending on workshop
items.cta: Get a tailored Nissan Silvia engine replacement quote today

items.question: What is the most common problem with SR20DET engines?
items.answer: NVCS rattle from worn solenoid or sprocket is the most reported SR20DET issue on S14/S15 models, followed by coil pack failure causing misfire under boost.
keyPoints: NVCS rattle appears 80k-120k miles, metallic ticking from cylinder head
Coil pack failure causes misfire under load, easy diagnostic scan
Head gasket failure common on high-boost or overheated engines
CAS failure can cause no-start conditions on high-mileage units
items.cta: Compare rebuilt SR20DET engine prices with NVCS upgrade

items.question: Is the CA18DET engine reliable for daily driving?
items.answer: The CA18DET can be reliable for daily use if maintained, but timing chain stretch after 80k miles and head gasket vulnerability require proactive maintenance or rebuilt replacement.
keyPoints: Timing chain tensioner upgrade recommended at 80k miles preventative
Head gasket failure risk increases with boost or poor cooling
Lighter than SR20DET, popular for budget drift builds
Rebuilt units with MLS gasket offer best long-term reliability
items.cta: Get quotes for CA18DET Nissan Silvia engine replacement

items.question: What's the difference between S14 and S15 SR20DET engines?
items.answer: S15 SR20DET features upgraded T28 turbo with inconel wheel, 444cc injectors (vs 370cc), and 247-250bhp output versus S14's 217bhp, plus refined ECU mapping.
keyPoints: S15 turbo more robust but same seal failure vulnerability
Larger injectors support higher power potential on S15
Both share NVCS VVT system and common failure points
S15 Spec R commands premium pricing for replacement engines
items.cta: Compare S14 vs S15 SR20DET engine replacement costs

items.question: Can I swap an RB25DET into my Nissan Silvia?
items.answer: Yes, RB25DET swaps into S13/S14/S15 are popular but require custom engine mounts, wiring harness adaptation, and ECU tuning from a specialist familiar with Silvia chassis.
keyPoints: RB25DET offers 250bhp straight-six smoothness and torque
Custom mounts and transmission adapter required for fitment
Wiring/ECU integration needs specialist knowledge
Budget £2,500-£4,200 for rebuilt RB25DET plus swap labour
items.cta: Enquire about RB25DET Nissan Silvia swap engine availability

items.question: How long should a rebuilt SR20DET engine last?
items.answer: A properly rebuilt SR20DET with upgraded components should deliver 100k+ miles of reliable service, with NVCS and coil pack maintenance extending lifespan further.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines include new bearings, seals, MLS gasket as standard
NVCS solenoid service recommended every 60k miles preventative
Coil packs should be replaced proactively at 80k miles
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt units
items.cta: Get quotes for SR20DET rebuilt engine with warranty

items.question: What causes white smoke from a Nissan Silvia exhaust?
items.answer: White smoke typically indicates head gasket failure (coolant burning) or turbo seal failure (oil burning) on SR20DET/CA18DET engines - compression test diagnoses root cause.
keyPoints: Sweet smell suggests coolant burning: head gasket failure likely
Blue-tinged smoke suggests oil burning: turbo seal failure probable
Compression test and leak-down test confirm diagnosis
Rebuilt engine with MLS gasket resolves head gasket issues permanently
items.cta: Compare Nissan Silvia engine replacement prices for head gasket failure

items.question: Is it worth rebuilding a high-mileage S13 Silvia engine?
items.answer: For clean-chassis S13 models, rebuilding CA18DET/SR20DET engines is cost-effective versus replacement; for rusted or modified cars, consider part-out value versus rebuild investment.
keyPoints: Rebuild CA18DET: £1,500-£2,200 versus used £800-£1,800
S13 chassis values rising; quality rebuild protects investment
Factor in timing chain, head gasket, turbo condition in decision
Specialist rebuilders offer warranty protection versus breaker units
items.cta: Get a tailored rebuild quote for your Nissan Silvia S13

items.question: What warranty comes with a replacement Nissan Silvia engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt and reconditioned Nissan Silvia engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty; used engines typically carry 3-6 months.
keyPoints: 12-month warranty standard on rebuilt/reconditioned units
Warranty covers parts and labour at supplying specialist
Used engine warranties shorter; verify terms before purchase
Extended warranty options available for forged performance builds
items.cta: Compare Nissan Silvia engine warranty terms from UK specialists

items.question: How do I identify my Nissan Silvia engine code?
items.answer: Engine code is stamped on the block near the bellhousing; SR20DET, CA18DET, RB20DET or RB25DET will be clearly marked. Enter your reg above for instant DVLA verification.
keyPoints: SR20DET: 2.0L turbo, most common replacement engine
CA18DET: 1.8L turbo, early S13 models only
RB codes: straight-six swaps, require custom fitment
DVLA lookup via registration confirms exact variant and engine
items.cta: Enter your reg to identify your Nissan Silvia engine code instantly
